description: Get the logs of a Kubernetes Pod (or workload name that will be resolved to a pod) in a namespace. Output is plain text, matching kubernetes-mcp-server pods_log. The line_count field tells you the total number of log lines returned. Analyze ALL of them, but summarize the results unless the user explicitly asks for the raw output. Do not omit any error or warning lines.

container.description: Optional. Name of the Pod container to get the logs from.

format.description: Output formatting for chat. 'codeblock' wraps logs in ~~~ fences (recommended). 'plain' returns raw text like kubernetes-mcp-server pods_log.

meshCluster.description: Optional Istio mesh cluster name from ossm_list_mesh_clusters (e.g. west). When omitted, Kiali defaults to its home cluster.

name.description: Name of the Pod to get the logs from. If it does not exist, it will be treated as a workload name and a running pod will be selected.

namespace.description: Namespace to get the Pod logs from

previous.description: Optional. Return previous terminated container logs

severity.description: Optional severity filter applied client-side. Accepts 'ERROR', 'WARN' or combinations like 'ERROR,WARN'.

tail.description: Number of lines to retrieve from the end of the logs (Optional, defaults to 50). Cannot exceed 200 lines.

workload.description: Optional. Workload name override (used when name lookup fails).

name: ossm_get_logs
title: Workload: Logs

description: Retrieves the high-level health, topology, and environment details of the Istio service mesh. Returns multi-cluster control plane status (istiod), data plane namespace health (including ambient mesh status), observability stack health (Prometheus, Grafana...), and component connectivity. Use this tool as the first step to diagnose mesh-wide issues, verify Istio/Kiali versions, or check overall health before drilling into specific workloads.
name: ossm_get_mesh_status
title: Get Mesh Status

description: Returns service-to-service traffic topology, dependencies, and network metrics (throughput, response time, mTLS) for the specified namespaces. Use this to diagnose routing issues, latency, or find upstream/downstream dependencies.

graphType.description: Granularity of the graph. 'app' aggregates by app name, 'versionedApp' separates by versions, 'workload' maps specific pods/deployments. Default: versionedApp.

meshCluster.description: Optional Istio mesh cluster name from ossm_list_mesh_clusters (e.g. west). When omitted, Kiali defaults to its home cluster.

namespaces.description: Comma-separated list of namespaces to map

name: ossm_get_mesh_traffic_graph
title: Get Mesh Traffic Graph

description: Returns a compact JSON summary of Istio metrics (latency quantiles, traffic trends, throughput, payload sizes) for the given resource.

byLabels.description: Comma-separated list of labels to group metrics by (e.g., 'source_workload,destination_service'). Optional

meshCluster.description: Optional Istio mesh cluster name from ossm_list_mesh_clusters (e.g. west). When omitted, Kiali defaults to its home cluster.

quantiles.description: Comma-separated list of quantiles for histogram metrics (e.g., '0.5,0.95,0.99'). Optional

rateInterval.description: Rate interval for metrics (e.g., '1m', '5m'). Optional, defaults to '10m'

reporter.description: Metrics reporter(s). Comma-separated list of: 'source', 'destination', 'waypoint', or the special value 'both' (no reporter filter). Optional, defaults to 'source'. Example: 'source,waypoint'

resourceName.description: Name of the resource to get metrics for

resourceType.description: Type of resource to get metrics

step.description: Step between data points in seconds (e.g., '15'). Optional, defaults to 15 seconds

name: ossm_get_metrics
title: Get Metrics for a Resource

description: Returns a human-readable text summary with current Pod CPU/memory usage (from Prometheus) compared to Kubernetes requests/limits (from the Pod spec). Useful to answer questions like 'Is this workload using too much memory?'

meshCluster.description: Optional Istio mesh cluster name from ossm_list_mesh_clusters (e.g. west). When omitted, Kiali defaults to its home cluster.

podName.description: Kubernetes Pod name. If workloadName is provided, the tool will attempt to resolve a Pod from that workload first.

queryTime.description: Optional end timestamp (RFC3339) for the query. Defaults to now.

timeRange.description: Time window used to compute CPU rate (Prometheus duration like '5m', '10m', '1h', '1d'). Defaults to '10m'.

workloadName.description: Kubernetes Workload name (e.g. Deployment/StatefulSet/etc). Tool will look up the workload and pick one of its Pods. If not found, it will fall back to treating this value as a podName.

name: ossm_get_pod_performance
title: Get Pod Performance

description: Fetches a list of resources OR retrieves detailed data for a specific resource. If 'resourceName' is omitted, it returns a list. If 'resourceName' is provided, it returns details for that specific resource.

meshCluster.description: Optional Istio mesh cluster name from ossm_list_mesh_clusters (e.g. west). When omitted, Kiali defaults to its home cluster.

namespaces.description: Comma-separated list of namespaces to query (e.g., 'bookinfo' or 'bookinfo,default'). If not provided, it will query across all accessible namespaces.

resourceName.description: Optional. The specific name of the resource. If left empty, the tool returns a list of all resources of the specified type. If provided, the tool returns deep details for this specific resource.

resourceType.description: The type of resource to query. Use 'app' for Kiali applications (grouped by the Kubernetes 'app' label). Use 'argoapp' for ArgoCD Application CRDs (requires ArgoCD installed and the Kiali service account must have read permissions on applications.argoproj.io).

name: ossm_get_resource_details
title: List or Resource Details

description: Fetches a single distributed trace by trace_id and returns its call hierarchy (service tree with duration, status, and nested calls). Use this after list_traces to drill into a specific trace.

traceId.description: Trace ID to fetch and summarize. If provided, namespace/service_name are ignored.

name: ossm_get_trace_details
title: Get Trace Details

description: Returns the list of Istio mesh clusters that Kiali can access. Each entry includes its name and whether it is the home cluster (where Kiali is deployed). Call this tool before using meshCluster on other Kiali tools when the target cluster is unknown.
name: ossm_list_mesh_clusters
title: List Mesh Clusters

description: Lists distributed traces for a service in a namespace. Returns a summary (namespace, service, total_found, avg_duration_ms) and a list of traces with id, duration_ms, spans_count, root_op, slowest_service, has_errors. Use get_trace_details with a trace id to get full hierarchy.

errorOnly.description: If true, only consider traces that contain errors. Default false.

limit.description: Maximum number of traces to return. Default 10.

lookbackSeconds.description: How far back to search. Default 600 (10m).

meshCluster.description: Optional Istio mesh cluster name from ossm_list_mesh_clusters (e.g. west). When omitted, Kiali defaults to its home cluster.

serviceName.description: Service name to search traces for (required). Returns multiple traces up to limit.

name: ossm_list_traces
title: List Traces by Service Name

annotations.title: Manage Istio, Gateway API, and Inference API Config: Create, Patch, Delete

description: Create, patch, or delete Istio, Gateway API, and Inference API config. Supports Istio resources (networking.istio.io, security.istio.io), Gateway API resources (gateway.networking.k8s.io), and Inference API resources (inference.networking.k8s.io) when installed on the cluster. For list and get (read-only) use manage_istio_config_read.

data.description: JSON or YAML data for the resource. Required for create and patch actions. For create, you can provide partial content (e.g. only spec) and it will be merged onto a valid template with defaults. Arrays (like servers, http, etc.) are REPLACED entirely, so include ALL elements you want.

group.description: API group of the Istio object. Use 'gateway.networking.k8s.io' for Gateway API resources. Use 'inference.networking.k8s.io' for Inference API resources.

meshCluster.description: Optional Istio mesh cluster name from ossm_list_mesh_clusters (e.g. west). When omitted, Kiali defaults to its home cluster.

version.description: API version. Use 'v1' for all resource types.

name: ossm_manage_istio_config
title: Manage Istio, Gateway API, and Inference API Config: Create, Patch, Delete

annotations.title: Manage Istio, Gateway API, and Inference API Config: List or Get

description: Read Istio, Gateway API, and Inference API config. 'list' groups by namespace→'group/version/kind'→{valid:[...],invalid:[...]} where valid/invalid arrays contain resource names; omit group/kind to retrieve ALL config types in a single call. Supports Istio (networking.istio.io, security.istio.io), Gateway API (gateway.networking.k8s.io), and Inference API (inference.networking.k8s.io) when installed. 'get' returns full YAML. For writes use manage_istio_config.

group.description: API group of the Istio object. Required ONLY for 'get' action. For 'list', OMIT group and kind to retrieve ALL config types in a single call. Use 'gateway.networking.k8s.io' for Gateway API resources. Use 'inference.networking.k8s.io' for Inference API resources.

kind.description: Kind of the Istio object. Required ONLY for 'get' action. For 'list', OMIT to return all kinds at once — do NOT call separately for each kind.

meshCluster.description: Optional Istio mesh cluster name from ossm_list_mesh_clusters (e.g. west). When omitted, Kiali defaults to its home cluster.

namespace.description: Namespace containing the Istio object. For 'list', if not provided, returns objects across all namespaces. For 'get', required.

object.description: Name of the Istio object. Required for 'get' action.

serviceName.description: Filter Istio configurations (VirtualServices, DestinationRules, and their referenced Gateways) that affect a specific service. Only applicable for 'list' action

version.description: API version. Use 'v1' for all resource types. Required for 'get' action.

name: ossm_manage_istio_config_read
title: Manage Istio, Gateway API, and Inference API Config: List or Get
